title: Crisp Fluffy Peruvian Pinto Beans Bowl
description: A smoky, fluffy bowl of pinto beans infused with Peruvian flavors, served over a bed of binchotan-charcoal toasted beans, perfect for a low-calorie lunch.
instructions: Rinse pinto beans thoroughly under cold water and soak them overnight or for at least 8 hours.
Drain the beans and combine them with 4 cups of water in a large pot; bring to a gentle boil over medium-high heat.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer the beans until tender, about 45 minutes to 1 hour.
While the beans cook, place two binchotan charcoal discs in a small heatproof container and light them using a long match; let them burn until glowing embers form.
Once the beans are tender, drain any excess liquid and transfer the beans to a heatproof bowl.
Drizzle 1 tbsp olive oil over the beans, then sprinkle 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp pepper; toss gently to coat.
Spoon the hot beans onto a serving platter, and top with freshly squeezed lime juice, diced red onion, and chopped cilantro.
Serve immediately, or let the heat transfer from the binchotan charcoal create a subtle smoky aroma that enhances the fluffy texture.
